title: Perform Daily Preventive Inspection Of The Operator Station
summary: Perform the documented daily preventive inspection of the operator station by checking for controller damage, confirming stacklights are operational, and verifying the chute is free of debris such as stuck labels.
when_to_use: Use during the daily preventive maintenance inspection for the operator station.
not_for: Detailed stacklight troubleshooting or electrical repair
Debris removal methods not documented in the source
Tipper maintenance or robot maintenance procedures

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Perform the documented daily preventive inspection items for the operator station and escalate issues when abnormal conditions are found.

safety_notes: This source provides inspection points only and does not provide corrective maintenance steps.
Do not perform repair or non-documented clearing methods based on this runbook alone.
access_or_tools_needed: Physical access to the operator station
Visual access to the controller, stacklights, and chute

access_required: Access to the operator station area
system_preconditions: Operator station is available for visual inspection
external_references: P40A (V2.0) user manual for P40 robot maintenance, as referenced by the daily preventive maintenance overview
Lifecycle Performance Services (LPS) contact for tipper maintenance, as referenced by the daily preventive maintenance overview

visual_references.description: Reference photo of the operator station showing the chute, stacklights, HMI, and overall station layout.
Reference photo showing the rotary station under the chute to help orient the chute inspection area.
Reference image showing an operator station HMI screen; useful only for identifying operator station context, not for this inspection procedure.

steps.title: Go to the operator station
steps.purpose: Position the inspector at the correct equipment for the daily preventive inspection.
steps.instruction: Go to the operator station to perform the daily preventive maintenance inspection.
steps.expected_result: You are at the operator station and can visually inspect the controller, stacklights, and chute.
steps.healthy_condition: The operator station and its inspection points are accessible for visual inspection.
steps.failure_condition: The operator station or required inspection points cannot be accessed or identified.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Use the operator station reference photo to identify the chute, stacklights, and overall station layout.
Use the rotary station photo to orient yourself to the area under the chute.

stop_or_escalate_if: The operator station cannot be safely accessed for inspection.
The required inspection points cannot be identified from the available source material.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: 7.1.1: OPERATOR STATION Inspect the operator station for the following: Damage to the controller. Stacklights are operational. Chute is free of debris (e.g., stuck labels).
Figure 3-1 appears on page 24 in section optisweep_operation_and_maintenance_manual_final_1_3_2_operator_station.

steps.title: Inspect the controller for damage
steps.purpose: Verify that the controller does not show visible damage during the daily preventive inspection.
steps.healthy_condition: The controller shows no visible damage.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Use the operator station reference image to identify the operator station area while locating inspection points.

stop_or_escalate_if: The controller cannot be adequately inspected.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: 7.1.1: OPERATOR STATION Inspect the operator station for the following: Damage to the controller.

steps.title: Verify stacklights are operational
steps.purpose: Confirm the operator station stacklights are operational as required by the daily preventive inspection.
steps.instruction: Verify that the stacklights are operational.
steps.failure_condition: One or more stacklights are not operational or their condition cannot be confirmed.

actions.label: Check stacklight operation
actions.exact_action: Verify that the stacklights are operational.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Look at the top of the frame on either side of the chute where the stacklights are mounted.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stacklight condition cannot be confirmed from inspection.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: 7.1.1: OPERATOR STATION Inspect the operator station for the following: Stacklights are operational.
Nearby text states that the station includes two stacklights mounted on the top of the frame on either side of the chute.

steps.title: Inspect the chute for debris
steps.purpose: Verify that the chute is free of debris, including stuck labels, as part of the daily preventive inspection.
steps.instruction: Inspect the chute and confirm it is free of debris, including stuck labels.
steps.expected_result: The chute is free of debris, including stuck labels.
steps.healthy_condition: No debris or stuck labels are present in the chute.
steps.failure_condition: Debris or stuck labels are present, or the chute condition cannot be confirmed.

actions.label: Check chute cleanliness
actions.exact_action: Inspect the chute and confirm it is free of debris, including stuck labels.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Identify the chute in the operator station reference image and inspect the corresponding physical chute area.
Use the rotary station image to orient the chute area above the rotary station.

stop_or_escalate_if: Debris or stuck labels are found and cannot be cleared using source-approved methods.
The chute cannot be adequately inspected.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: 7.1.1: OPERATOR STATION Inspect the operator station for the following: Chute is free of debris (e.g., stuck labels).
Figure 3-1 identifies the chute as an operator station feature.

success_criteria: Chute is confirmed free of debris, including stuck labels.
healthy_conditions: Operator station inspection points are accessible.
Chute is free of debris and stuck labels.
failure_conditions: Debris or stuck labels are present in the chute.
Any required inspection point cannot be adequately inspected.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if controller damage is observed.
Escalate if stacklights are not operational.
Escalate if chute debris cannot be cleared using source-approved methods.
Escalate when corrective action is required, because the source provides inspection points only and does not provide corrective actions.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Overall operator station layout, including chute and stacklights.
Area beneath the chute to help orient chute inspection.
Operator station context only; not required to execute this inspection.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: 7.1.1: OPERATOR STATION Inspect the operator station for the following: Damage to the controller. Stacklights are operational. Chute is free of debris (e.g., stuck labels).

missing_details: The source does not provide a documented inspection method for proving stacklights are operational.
The source does not provide corrective actions for controller damage, stacklight failure, or chute debris.
The source does not provide a documented debris-clearing method.
The source does not specify whether production must be stopped for this inspection.
The source does not specify whether LOTO is required for this inspection.
The source does not provide an estimated completion time.
The source does not explicitly assign the task to a named role; L2_support is inherited from the candidate.
